description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a large, convex glass screen framed by a thick, black bezel. The front panel features two prominent rotary dials and a small speaker grille, all set within a rectangular black section to the right of the screen. The casing is finished in a woodgrain veneer, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance, with the wood pattern visible on the top and sides. The back of the television is made of black plastic with ventilation slits and a protruding compartment for internal components, as well as a label and port cutouts. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of analog CRT televisions from the 1970s or 1980s. This television would likely be found in older urban environments, garages, or as discarded electronics on the street.
description: A vintage, wood-paneled CRT television with a convex glass screen, black bezel, and rotary dials on the front.
description_view_0: The back view shows a black plastic panel with ventilation slits, a protruding compartment, and a label, highlighting the technical and connection area.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the woodgrain finish and the depth of the television's body, emphasizing its boxy, retro profile.
description_view_2: The front view reveals the convex glass screen, two rotary dials, a speaker grille, and the black bezel, showcasing the main interactive features.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the woodgrain finish and the television's overall thickness.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick black plastic bezel with a matte finish, rectangular shape, and integrated rotary dials and speaker grille.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two rotary dials and a button arranged vertically on the front right panel.
